Skip to contents
ato 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Canonical replications: top 1% share, corporate ETR, tax gap trend
    • Interop with grattan and taxstats: costing a hypothetical reform
    • Methodology: hand-computed worked examples
    • Panels, harmonisation, reconciliation, real terms, per-capita
    • Reproducibility workflow: snapshot, manifest, SHA-256, Zenodo
  • Changelog

Authors and Citation

Authors

  • Charles Coverdale. Author, maintainer.

Citation

Source: inst/CITATION

Coverdale, C. (2026). ato: Download and Tidy Australian Taxation Office Data. R package version 0.1.0. https://cran.r-project.org/package=ato

@Manual{,
  title = {ato: Download and Tidy Australian Taxation Office Data},
  author = {Charles Coverdale},
  year = {2026},
  note = {R package version 0.1.0},
  url = {https://cran.r-project.org/package=ato},
}

Atkinson, A.B. and Leigh, A. (2007). "The Distribution of Top Incomes in Australia." Economic Record, 83(262), 247-261. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4932.2007.00412.x

@Article{,
  title = {The Distribution of Top Incomes in Australia},
  author = {Anthony B. Atkinson and Andrew Leigh},
  journal = {Economic Record},
  year = {2007},
  volume = {83},
  number = {262},
  pages = {247--261},
  doi = {10.1111/j.1475-4932.2007.00412.x},
}

Parsonage, H. (2019). taxstats: 2 per cent Individual Sample File from the Australian Taxation Office. R package (DRAT). https://github.com/HughParsonage/taxstats

@Manual{,
  title = {taxstats: 2 per cent Individual Sample File from the Australian Taxation Office},
  author = {Hugh Parsonage},
  year = {2019},
  note = {R package (DRAT)},
  url = {https://github.com/HughParsonage/taxstats},
}

Parsonage, H. (2026). grattan: Perform Common Quantitative Tasks for Australian Analysts. R package. https://cran.r-project.org/package=grattan

@Manual{,
  title = {grattan: Perform Common Quantitative Tasks for Australian Analysts},
  author = {Hugh Parsonage},
  year = {2026},
